label: Move-out responsibility dispute
eventText:
Mari sent a long OFW message Monday morning claiming she'd put 80+ hours into clean-out of the townhouse and that she'd be sending me a $7K invoice for repairs and labor through her counsel. She accused me of abandoning the place in late January and doing nothing for move-out. This contradicts the actual written record on OFW so I documented it back to her in writing.

The actual sequence: on January 12, Mari emailed the landlords asking to be removed from the lease. On January 27, she told them 'Kyle will be responsible for the rent.' Vithya (the landlord) responded that we are both responsible until the lease is properly terminated. On March 1, Mari told me in writing on OFW that she was leaving the move-out responsibilities to me, and she threatened to confront the junk-removal crew I had scheduled, which forced me to cancel it. I asked her to let me know when I could reschedule. I never heard back.

On March 4, she gave me two options: (1) she hires a contractor and we split it, or (2) we let the landlords use their own contractors. On March 7, I chose option 2. She disagreed and hired her own contractor without my approval, and managed the entire scope, timeline, and cost without running any of it by me.

Reality of what I actually did: I paid for a professional deep cleaning out of pocket. I paid rent in full each month even though both of our names are on the lease. I had junk removal scheduled and ready to go until she blocked it (in writing, on OFW, by stating she'd confront the workers if they showed up). I've been responsive on OFW every time she's reached out.

I told her that since she hired the contractor herself without my approval of scope or cost, the payment arrangement is between her and the contractor. Any reimbursement for shared obligations should go through our attorneys.

Evidence to flag: OFW messages from Jan 12, Jan 27, Mar 1, Mar 4, Mar 7. Landlord email confirming joint liability.

label: Junk removal coordination + schedule swap for NJ trip
eventText:
Two things today.

First, junk removal. Our lease ends Friday March 27 and there's still stuff in the townhouse garage. I asked Mari if I could schedule junk removal for Wednesday or Thursday. She said 'feel free to do it whenever works for you' but the contractor she hired needed to fix an exterior pipe and she didn't think they should overlap. I offered Friday but she said she had a work meeting and a playdate with Josie. We landed on Thursday morning. They're coming Thursday.

Second, schedule swap for next week. My brother is having a baby on Tuesday March 31 (scheduled C-section) and I want to take Josie up to NJ to be there with the family. I asked Mari for two extra nights to make that work. She was going to DC for the cherry blossom festival with friends so couldn't take Josie those extra nights. We negotiated and landed on: I take Josie until Friday morning April 3 (drop off at daycare so Josie has a smooth transition rather than a sudden switch right before bedtime), then Mari has Josie from Friday through Monday April 6 morning to balance it out. Then back to regular schedule.

This was actually a productive cooperative exchange on the schedule swap. Both of us flexed and we landed on something workable for the family event.

label: Josie diarrhea + stomachache report
eventText:
Mari messaged me at 9:29 AM saying Josie had a pretty bad diarrhea last evening (Tuesday March 24) after daycare and that Josie said her tummy hurt. Mari asked what Josie had eaten the day before to check whether something was irritating her or whether this was a recurring sensitivity. I responded that nothing was out of the ordinary, that it was her normal lunch and breakfast.

Mari pushed back: 'What is normal lunch and breakfast, I don't live in your house. Do you care to share or are you intentionally not sharing? This is not even about you and me, I'm trying to care for Josie.'

Flagging this as an early-warning data point. About 10 days later (April 4) Josie ended up at the children's hospital ER with a GI infection, IV fluids, and overnight stay. This stomachache + diarrhea episode may have been an early sign.

label: Move-out walkthrough findings + Saturday transition
eventText:
Two things today.

Landlord walkthrough findings. I did the walkthrough and sent Mari the punch list: garage fridge had rotten food and smelled terrible (needed cleaning out), garage needed sweeping, broken pipe was 'fixed' but the work was shoddy and the end of the pipe is cracked, my old bathroom seems to have been skipped by the contractor (cracking paint in the tub, black caulk that needs replacing), leftover caulk on the new vanity. Overall the landlord was happy but those items needed fixing before new tenants moved in Saturday. I'd be in NJ for the week so I asked Mari to coordinate. She refused and told me to call my cleaner back since I paid for them. She said she'd already put 80+ hours and $7K and was 'leaving the rest' to me. I'd already be out of town.

Saturday transition. Scheduled for 5pm. I checked in at 4:44pm. Mari was at the science museum with Josie and ran late — she said Josie didn't want to leave, museum was about to close, she'd be a few minutes late. She arrived at the apartment around 5:17pm and dropped Josie off without further conflict. She mentioned she'd be home early evening and to let her know if I wanted to come by for Josie's bag.

The next day (Sunday March 29) I texted asking about the inside part of Josie's coat. Mari said everything was packed in a bag and to let her know when I wanted it. I picked it up around 6:45pm Sunday before heading up to NJ Monday.

label: NJ trip with Josie for brother's baby
eventText:
Just got back from a week in NJ with Josie for my brother's scheduled C-section (Tuesday March 31). The whole family was up there. Per the schedule swap I negotiated with Mari last week, I had Josie from Monday March 30 through today.

Really good week. Josie was in a great mood the entire trip with high energy. She got a ton of sleep, including a long nap yesterday. She loved being around extended family.

We're driving back to RVA and should arrive around 4:30 today. I texted Mari at 11:21 AM offering to bring Josie to her around 5 if that works. I noted she'd had one extra night with me this week and I could pick Josie up from Mari Sunday to get back on track. Mari replied at 11:51 AM that she couldn't get Josie today — she had a prior work engagement she couldn't bail on — and clarified it wasn't out of malice. I'll keep Josie tonight and bring her to Mari for the rest of the originally agreed-upon window.

No conflict on this one — both of us flexed for each other to make the family event work.

label: Josie at children's hospital ER with GI infection
eventText:
Josie was hospitalized today. Mari took her to the children's hospital ER around midday for persistent vomiting (including throwing up water), pale lips, and signs of dehydration. Josie told Mari her tummy hurt and that she wanted to see the doctor.

This morning around 10:27 AM Mari first messaged me saying Josie had been sick since last night and started puking in the morning, that her lips were pale. Josie pukes on water so she hadn't had anything. Mari was giving her tiny amounts of electrolytes. Mari noted this was the second incident — Josie had a stomachache and diarrhea last Friday evening (March 24) too.

I swung by Mari's place to pick up the townhouse remote and keys around 11:51 AM. By 1:50 PM Mari texted that she'd taken Josie to the children's hospital ER. They gave her Tylenol and something for the vomiting (Zofran). They suspected GI infection but tested for more to be safe — blood draw, urine test, blood sugar.

The doctor flagged that when a child is suddenly extremely thirsty and pees a lot, they screen for diabetes — they checked Josie's blood sugar. Her blood sugar was low so they put in an IV with sugar and fluids. The paramedic was there to put the IV in.

Around 4:32 PM Mari texted that they were staying overnight. Blood work showed pretty bad dehydration and low blood sugar, and they wanted to make sure her labs came back to normal before discharge.

I was at my apartment. I checked in via OFW a few times asking how Josie was doing and what was happening. Mari handled the entire hospitalization solo. I asked her to keep me posted.

Evidence to flag: ER discharge papers, billing records from children's hospital, Mari's photos she sent at 11:37 AM (jpg, PXL_20260404_153429696.jpg), the OFW thread itself.

label: Josie discharged, kept by Mari Sunday, picked up Sunday afternoon
eventText:
Mari and Josie got discharged after lunchtime today. Mari told me at 7:11 AM Josie was doing better. By 1:13 PM they were back home. Josie was still going through the viral infection — condition going up and down. The doctor said she might still be contagious to other kids so Mari planned to keep her home from school Monday.

Doctor's care plan that Mari shared:
- Motrin/Tylenol if Josie has stomach pain or fever
- Zofran if she starts vomiting again (CVS hadn't filled yet)
- No sugar — makes GI infection worse (Josie understood this and didn't ask for sweets)
- Bland food (nothing too salty, spicy, greasy) for a week or two
- Possible diarrhea for a week or two
- Keep her hydrated — small frequent sips, not big amounts
- Watch for signs of dehydration: sunken eyes, pale/dry lips

I took Josie around 4:30 PM. Mari brought her over after dining at the Jefferson with Josie (Mari said she'd promised Josie a vacation there after the hospitalization). I took Josie to see my mom and aunt Vicki. We all hung out together — Josie in good spirits, mostly recovered by then. I got her home to bed.

At 4:23 PM Mari mentioned she was starting to feel really horrible herself, possibly catching what Josie had, and asked if I could be backup tomorrow evening if she ended up at the hospital.

Later that evening I sent Mari a quick OFW about lunchbox cleanup — leftover food was sitting in Josie's lunchbox for several days before getting back to the other parent and damaging the containers. I asked if we could agree that whoever has Josie cleans the lunchbox out same day. Mari took it as me starting conflict — said she'd just done 48 hours of solo caregiving including overnight ER and asked me not to start fights. I clarified I wasn't accusing, just trying to align. She refused to engage further: 'Why don't you just let it go?'

label: Mari sick, Kyle takes Josie an extra day
eventText:
Mari texted at 9:22 AM saying she was having GI infection-like symptoms herself — almost certainly caught it from Josie. She asked if I could take care of Josie today since she wasn't in good shape. I agreed, with the condition that she take Josie on Tuesday next week instead of Wednesday to balance it out. Mari said yes immediately: 'Absolutely, thankyou.'

This was a cooperative exchange. Both of us flexed for the other when sick.

label: Saturday drop-off — clothing/shoes dispute
eventText:
Saturday drop-off. Mari texted at 8:41 AM that she had a birthday party with Josie running till 5pm so they'd be a little late. She also asked me to return her stuff at drop-off — at least 2 pairs of Josie's shoes were at my place leaving them without weather-appropriate shoes that week. Possibly missing underwear, socks, shirts, pants and dress.

I asked her to be specific about what shoes and clothes since I lose track of who bought what. She listed sparkly Crocs and dress shoes for the shoes; said she didn't know exactly which clothes I was holding but that I'd 'only recently started buying clothes for her from Target' so anything non-Target or pre-January 2026 would be hers. Asked me to cross-check with my family.

They arrived at 5:30 PM. Drop-off itself was fine — no on-site conflict, just the back-and-forth in the messages.

label: Josie surgery info + Mari's unilateral schedule proposal
eventText:
Mari sent over the surgery info at 5:50 PM after talking to the nurse. Surgery is scheduled for 9:30-11 AM Friday April 17. Check in at 8 AM on the 2nd floor of children's hospital. Only 2 adults allowed, no kids. Recovery: bed rest 24-48 hrs, anesthesia takes ~24 hrs to wear off, pain sustained for at least 48 hrs requiring hands-on care, no physical activity that may affect the incision for a week. Josie out of school for a week.

Then Mari attached a custody schedule proposal that she said she'd already shared with the GAL (Ms. Imholt):
- Exchange Mari to Kyle on Wednesday April 22 at 10am
- Exchange Kyle to Mari on Wednesday April 29 after the post-op visit at 1:30pm
- Back to regular schedule from April 29 or new schedule

She framed it as 'recovery continuity at a single location for the first 72 hours' and 'equitable a-week-with-each-parent.'

I counter-proposed at 7:06 PM. Keep the regular schedule with one tweak: I pick up Josie Sunday around 11-noon instead of Saturday 5pm. That gives her the first ~40 hours with Mari for the anesthesia and peak pain window, and Sunday is family day at my mom's where she'd have grandma and extra hands around to keep her comfortable through the recovery week. Mom was already prepping the guest room.

Mari refused at 7:16 PM: 'I don't feel comfortable transporting her down to Chester after 40 hours of surgery. Parental concerns as her mother should come first and be respected before extended family.' She also said she'd heard from Ms. Imholt that I no longer want Sunday as my designated family day, and asked to verify.

I clarified at 8:15 PM: Sunday pickup makes sense, Josie will be well taken care of from there through the rest of the no-school week, and that this is an OK time to loop in counsel and Ms. Imholt — please CC me and include the message thread.

Mari escalated at 8:43 PM: 'I am not comfortable with you transferring her to Chester, further away from the hospital. I will be comfortable with that only after 7 days when risk of infection gets lower.' Said she'd let her counsel handle communication.

This is the start of Mari unilaterally proposing to override our agreed schedule under the umbrella of medical necessity, despite no actual surgeon-issued travel restriction.

Evidence to flag: this OFW thread (Josie surgery information), surgeon's discharge instructions, GAL communications via Ms. Imholt, counsel-to-counsel letter from Mari's lawyer (Richard) to mine (Clayton).

label: Surgery day — address never sent, Josie home post-op
eventText:
Surgery day. The plan per Mari's earlier message was check-in 8 AM at children's hospital 2nd floor, surgery 9:30-11 AM. Only 2 adults allowed.

The night before (April 17, 1:13 AM) I asked Mari to send over the location and timing info so I knew where to be. She replied at 6:07 AM 'Please see the first message on this thread. I sent you all I know.' That message had the timing and floor but not the address. I asked again at 8:33 AM: 'Hey Mari can you send the address please.' She didn't reply with the address.

Surgery happened. Josie went home with Mari post-op.

That evening at 7:07 PM I checked in: 'Heya, how's Josie girl holding up? Doing ok taking care of her?' Mari replied at 7:18 PM: Josie just had dinner, anesthesia was starting to wear off but she was still a bit disoriented and generally really tired. Pain was manageable with Motrin and Arnica (homeopathic pain relief — Mari said she'd send a bottle). Josie was doing well with quiet play at home. She didn't want to walk or do stairs so Mari had her in arms most of the day. They went on a long stroller walk in the afternoon.

Flag: address was not provided despite repeated requests. Surgeon issued no travel restrictions besides 'no riding toys for a week.'

label: Kyle reaffirms Sunday pickup; Mari refuses; counsel letter received
eventText:
Saturday. I sent Mari an OFW at 12:33 PM laying out the plan: I'm picking Josie up Sunday between 11 and noon. That gives her about 48 hours with Mari post-surgery, which covers the roughest part of the anesthesia and pain window from what the surgeon said. Josie would have a chill recovery week with me and family. If the surgeon had specific post-op transport guidance I asked Mari to send it; I'd follow it. Said if we couldn't land on the same page, happy to let counsel and Ms. Imholt sort it.

Mari replied at 12:41 PM: 'Hey, please reach out to your counsel. My counsel sent him a letter yesterday after seeking guidance from Ms. Imholt. I don't feel comfortable transporting her to Chester, so that's a no for me.'

I clarified at 1:03 PM: the surgeon didn't mention any travel restrictions; the only restriction was no riding toys for a week. Chester is a normal drive Josie has done countless times. I told her if her counsel had specific post-op guidance from the surgeon, send it over. Plan was Sunday 11-noon pickup; if Clayton and Richard landed somewhere different by then, I'd follow counsel.

Mari escalated her rationale at 1:09 PM (also said it had been shared with Ms. Imholt):
- 'Josie made it very clear before and after her surgery that she constantly needed my physical and emotional reassurance for comfort... she kept reaching for me even if you took her from me'
- The care team confident in her ability to spot infection because she's had a C-section
- She lives 2 mi from hospital vs Chester at 20 mi
- Her proposal ensures continuity, proximity to hospital, and equal split
- 'Josie expressed that she wants to stay with me'
- 'I will stick to my proposal unless my counsel suggests otherwise'

I replied at 1:18 PM: 'I hear you that you're planning to keep Josie past the end of our agreed-upon schedule. I disagree with that, but I'm not going to go back and forth on it today. I'll plan on Sunday pickup at 11. If you're not willing to exchange, I'll handle it through Clayton on Monday.'

Mari at 1:27 PM: 'Thanks, please work with your counsel. I'm focusing on what's best for Josie given her needs. I'd appreciate your willingness to cooperate with me for her care but rather than pushing for control during this unusual time. It's been very apparent that Josie wants mother's care during the hard time, which is very normal for this age.'

Flag: Mari's counsel sent a letter to my counsel Clayton on April 17 (Friday) after consulting with Ms. Imholt, the GAL. Need to follow up with Clayton to get a copy of that letter.

label: Sunday pickup attempt — Mari refused to release Josie
eventText:
Sunday — went to pick up Josie per the agreed schedule and Mari refused to release her.

10:26 AM Mari sent a video/photo of Josie having a hard night the night before — said the pain was harder yesterday since the meds from the hospital wore off. She said again she didn't feel comfortable transporting Josie further away from her, and that she didn't communicate directly with my family. She offered: 'If you don't want a week split where I take her till Wednesday morning and you take her till next Wednesday, happy to find a compromise; Tuesday one week split works too.'

I replied at 10:48 AM that I knew last night was rough, thanked her for taking care of Josie, said I'd keep Josie on the same pain med schedule Mari sent over and keep things very low-key at the house. If anything changed or the surgeon gave new guidance, send it my way and I'd adjust. Said I'd be there right around noon and could push to 1pm if Mari needed an extra hour.

Mari at 10:52 AM: 'Not comfortable with you transporting her 1.today 2. to Chester. Josie wants to be with me so I hope you understand that's the best interest of her, instead of pushing for control.'

I drove out anyway. At 12:13 PM I texted from the curb: 'Heya, I just pulled up. Can you bring Josie girl out? Along with the medications they gave you. parked out front.'

Mari at 12:34 PM: 'Please talk to your counsel. I expressed my concerns already and won't repeat over texts. Transporting her to Chester is a no for me until risk of infection gets lower, and I have legal custody over her for decision making. I've been very reasonable and flexible on accommodating to your schedule including your recent trip to NJ to see your family. I'm asking for the same reasonableness for Josie's care instead of you insisting control over her.'

I did not get Josie. Mari unilaterally executed her schedule change and denied me my parenting time. This is a denial of parenting time event — she invoked 'legal custody' to override the agreed-upon physical custody schedule despite no surgeon-issued restriction.

Evidence to flag: this OFW thread (Sunday pickup for Josie), the message thread, photos/video Mari sent of Josie's recovery, surgeon's actual discharge instructions (which said no riding toys for a week and nothing about travel).

label: Post-op visit rescheduled by hospital
eventText: Children's hospital called Mari to reschedule Josie's post-op visit. Originally scheduled for April 29 at 1:30 PM. New time: April 29 at 2:20 PM. Mari let me know at 11:43 AM.

label: Counter-offers around resumed transition
eventText:
Mari texted at 7:08 AM offering to drop Josie off Wednesday April 22 at 10 AM, and take her back next Wednesday April 29 after the 2:20 PM post-op visit. She framed it as 'previously proposed' and 'a little more than a week.' This is roughly the same proposal she made on April 15, just shifted by a couple of days because she'd unilaterally kept Josie through my parenting window already.

I replied at 3:52 PM: 'My counsel and I never agreed to that proposal. I have a normal workday scheduled based on our agreed-upon schedule and won't be available until 5. Let's do 5pm.'

Mari at 4:18 PM, sarcastic: 'Sounds like you can't be flexible with work for caregiving unless you delegate to your family. I'm more than happy to be the flexible one for Josie's care. 😊'

I asked again at 4:37 PM: 'Does 5pm work for you?'

Mari at 5:00 PM: 'Since my proposals never really work for you, you should propose an equitable transition after tomorrow. When in next week do you want me to pick up Josie from daycare?'

I at 5:27 PM: 'Can you bring her by at 5pm? You kept her through my entire 4 day parenting time, trying to make this work.'

Mari at 5:36 PM: 'Hey you haven't proposed anything about when to the transition back to me. I proposed 29th after post-op visit. But you keeps saying you don't agree to my proposal without any alternative. Does 29th work or do you want a different day? Open to work with you.'

No agreement landed by end of day. Mari was treating her unilateral proposal as the default and asking me to counter-propose.

label: Mari left Josie at daycare for 4:30 pickup, claimed work travel; calendar withdrawal
eventText:
This is the day Mari fully completed the unilateral schedule change.

Morning, 8:13 AM, Mari sent: 'Hi, since you don't have any counterproposal for the next transition, I'll assume your agreement on the 29th after the post op visit. For today's transition, I'll let you pick up Josie at the daycare at 430pm. Josie has been missing her friends a lot so I decided to bring her for half day. This will also avoid unnecessary interactions between you and me when we have a very difficult time communicating with each other. I hope we get to the point where we are cooperative, not being adversarial for Josie's best interest.'

So Mari decided unilaterally to:
1. Bring Josie to daycare for half-day (despite the medical-necessity argument she'd been using to deny me parenting time for the entire prior week)
2. Leave town during her own parenting time for a work engagement
3. Hand Josie off to me via daycare at 4:30 PM with no in-person exchange
4. Treat my refusal-to-counter as agreement to her April 29 transition back

I replied at 8:55 AM: 'Hey Mari, I don't agree to any of these changes and haven't agreed to you keeping her past our usual weekend transition. I cannot accommodate this day-of change. Can you drop her off at 5pm please?'

Mari at 9:01 AM doubled down: she said the proposal had been there since February 26, that she'd been continuously flexible while I refused, that she was the one stretching to accommodate. 'Josie will be at the daycare. Please coordinate pick up. Katy needs to leave at 430pm so maybe you can reach out to Rosemarie if you need Josie to stay longer. The pick up time is nothing unusual, it's something you've been available since Oct 2025.'

Mari at 9:05 AM: 'I have a work engagement out of town this afternoon. I've extended myself as much as I could to accommodate you. Please pick up Josie on time.'

I replied at 9:15 AM laying out the full picture: 'Your parenting time, per the only schedule we agreed upon, started yesterday at daycare pickup. We haven't agreed to any other arrangement. Your proposal was a proposal, and we never agreed upon it. I've attached the schedule (created by you). To be clear about what's happening: 1. you kept Josie through my parenting time. 2. you're leaving town during your own parenting time and leaving Josie at daycare to attend a work event. I'd appreciate it if you could at the very least bring Josie here at 5pm, before you leave town during your parenting time for a work event.'

Mari at 9:17 AM: 'I can't, out of town. I was already late because I accommodated to your work schedule and took care of Josie this morning. You still want to fight with me, instead of just figuring out the logistics for Josie. I am really not interested in arguing with you. Josie needs to be picked up at 430. I've done everything that I could to take care of her, try to work with you for her, and still accommodate to you.'

I ended up coordinating Josie's pickup from daycare myself.

Then at 1:29 PM Mari sent another message announcing she was withdrawing from our shared 'custody' Google calendar: 'Just letting you know that I will stop using the custody google calendar that I've been managing for both of us in order to protect myself from you weaponizing it. We should each manage our own calendar through open communication until we can be more cooperative and less adversarial.'

Net picture: Mari kept Josie 4 extra days through my parenting time citing medical necessity, then ended that argument by leaving town for work and depositing Josie at daycare while telling me to figure out logistics. She also pulled out of the shared coordination calendar.

Evidence to flag: this OFW thread, the schedule document Mari created and I attached in my 9:15 AM message, the shared 'custody' Google calendar showing her manage-then-withdraw, daycare pickup logs showing me retrieving Josie at 4:30 PM.
